{"id":"af-9n2x","title":"🤝 HANDOFF: SDT verdict validation complete","description":"## What was done
Implemented Signal Detection Theory verdict analysis in benchmark.py (commit c4a51e3):
- Fixed expected_verdict for 6 scenarios to match engine mechanics at each attempt count
- Added expected_disposition field (what SHOULD happen after full retry cycle)
- Added compute_verdict_analysis() — confusion matrix (sensitivity/specificity by tier)
- Added compute_optimal_threshold() — threshold sweep via Youden's J statistic
- New verdict analysis section in benchmark summary output
- Created experiments/sdt-explainer.html with interactive Chart.js visualizations

## Key findings
- Evaluator sensitivity is perfect (rank-order 36/36)
- Current pass_threshold=0.15 gives sensitivity=67%, specificity=100%
- Optimal threshold=0.47 gives 100%/100% (any threshold 0.23-0.72 works)
- The 0.50-point gap between tier-1 max (0.22) and tier-3 min (0.72) is huge

## Open questions for next session
1. Do we need more benchmark scenarios to better tune the threshold?
   - Current tier-1 has only 3 scenarios — thin sample
   - No scenarios near the decision boundary (gap 0.30-0.60 range)
   - Could add "good but messy" and "bad but polished" edge cases
   
2. What are the implications for the engine's pass_threshold?
   - Should we actually change pass_threshold from 0.15 to something in the optimal range?
   - Per-domain thresholds? Architecture review vs status report may need different bars
   - Or is this a follow-up after Phase 3?

3. Tier-2 scenarios (pm_borderline, tercuman_regression) live in the gap between tiers
   - They're not tested for verdict correctness (neither tier-1 nor tier-3)
   - Adding more tier-2 scenarios could help define the "retry zone"

## Files changed
- shepherd/benchmark.py (176 insertions, 7 deletions)
- experiments/sdt-explainer.html (new, not committed — visual explainer)

## State
- All 248 tests pass
- Pushed to main
- Local HTTP server was running on port 8766 for the explainer (may need restart)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"shepherd/book","owner":"70168+kingb@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-05T17:41:50Z","created_by":"shepherd/book","updated_at":"2026-03-05T17:41:50Z"}
